chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.590 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Justice Ms. Sana Akram Minhas. Qasoor Hussain..............Vs........................ The State 25.03.2026. Mr. Moazzam Hussain, Advocate for applicant Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested on 12.01.2026 by a police team of ANF, Karachi headed by Inspector Imdadullah, from main Abbasi Market Jokhia Goth, Model Colony, Malir, Karachi on suspicion and from him 505 grams of Charas and 105 grams of Ice were recovered, hence he was booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.01/2026 U/s 9 6(b), 9,, Sindh, CNS Act, 2025 of P.S. ANF, Malir, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel in defence submits that applicant has been falsely implicated in this case; he is first offender; the investigation is complete; challan has been submitted and the alleged offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C. 3. On the other hand, learned D.P.G. has opposed the bail. 4. We have considered arguments of the parties and perused material available on record. The offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C.; applicant is the first offender and there is no video recording of the incident as required under the law, more so since challan has been submitted, applicant is no more required for further investigation. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial.

Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
